campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans for a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary ordinance (local rule) #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant applications being submitted for Hamilton Avenue overcross project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which community organizations and events would receive city grant money for the upcoming fiscal year. They approved funding for specific groups, decided how much money each would get, and made some updates to the rules for how community events can apply for grants and close streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive city grant funding with individual dollar amounts
Allocated money for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated the Community Event and Street Closure Grant rules for clarity

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, YouTube, Zoom, and phone call-in options. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages to make it accessible to the city's diverse community.
keyTopics: Meeting access through Cable Channel 26, YouTube, and Zoom
Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Phone dial-in options for residents without internet access
Public comment restrictions (observation only, no public input at this meeting)

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council cancelled the zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026. They've rescheduled it for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 instead. If you have zoning questions or concerns, you'll need to wait for the April meeting to address them.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Zoning appeals delayed by two weeks

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting notes from their previous session and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services initiatives.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed for local projects
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding decisions
Affordable housing and human services funding priorities
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February 2026

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new initiatives. They discussed a program to help businesses catch up on licenses without penalties, plans to promote shopping at local stores, and ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor events and support retail shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get caught up without fines
Increased outreach to shopping centers and small business owners
Summer Shop Local campaign to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
Public-Private Partnerships for funding city events
New incentives and support for retail stores and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they addressed several postponed agenda items and held two public hearings. One hearing focused on approving a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of a county-wide program, and another gave residents a chance to comment on how the city plans to spend federal housing grant money for 2026-27.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026
Federal HUD housing grant spending plan (first draft)
Multiple agenda items delayed or reconsidered

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main items: a disaster relief agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities when emergencies happen, and a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Mimecast email security service contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report additions to meeting agenda
